requirements.summary: A minimal spec bundle used as a deterministic fixture for end-to-end smoke tests.

user_stories.capability: run a smoke test against a pre-canned fixture
user_stories.outcome: I can verify the spec materialization pipeline without calling an LLM

acceptance_criteria.given: a valid SpecBundle fixture is loaded
acceptance_criteria.then: requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md are written to the output directory

design.approach: Use a pre-canned JSON fixture to bypass LLM calls and drive the materialization pipeline deterministically in tests.

components.name: SpecBundle Fixture
components.purpose: Provides a minimal valid SpecBundle as static JSON for smoke tests.

design.data_model: No new persistent data models. Uses the existing SpecBundle Pydantic schema.
design.sequence:
1. Test loads fixture JSON.
2. Test parses it into a SpecBundle.
3. Materialization writes Markdown files.
4. Test asserts files exist and contain expected headers.

tasks.title: Create pre-canned SpecBundle fixture JSON
tasks.done_when: tests/e2e/fixtures/smoke_spec_bundle.json exists, contains a minimal valid SpecBundle, and passes schema validation when loaded in a unit test.
